libero.detail_notes: Avg is the mean of spatial, object, goal, and 10 suites (the standard 4-suite protocol used by ~82% of entries). Suite 90 is excluded as most papers skip it. Entries without suite breakdown may use different subsets — check notes. LIBERO-Plus, LIBERO-Pro, and LIBERO-Mem are separate benchmarks.

libero_plus.detail_notes: LIBERO-Plus (2510.13626). Robustness benchmark applying 7 perturbation dimensions (Camera, Robot, Language, Light, Background, Noise, Layout) to LIBERO tasks. Models are trained on standard LIBERO and evaluated zero-shot under perturbations. overall_score = paper-reported aggregate (averaged across all suite×perturbation cells, not simply the mean of 7 dimension scores).

libero_pro.detail_notes: LIBERO-Pro (2510.03827). Professional-difficulty variant of LIBERO. Separate benchmark from standard LIBERO.

libero_mem.detail_notes: LIBERO-Mem (2511.11478). Memory-augmented variant of LIBERO. Separate benchmark from standard LIBERO.

calvin.detail_notes: ABC→D split only (train on A/B/C, eval on D). Metric: avg completed subtasks in a chain of 5 (0–5), not success rate. Standard: 1000 eval chains.

simpler_env._note: Three independent evaluation dimensions. Do NOT average across them.
simpler_env.detail_notes: 3 independent evaluation dimensions (Google Robot VM, Google Robot VA, WidowX VM). Scores should never be averaged across them. Google Robot VM and VA are both standardized to the 3-task average (Pick Coke Can, Move Near, Open/Close Drawer) for consistent ranking. Papers reporting 4 tasks (adding Place Apple in Drawer) have the 4th task shown in sub-scores; original 4-task averages are recorded in Notes. All task_scores keys must end with _vm or _va to indicate the evaluation protocol.

maniskill2.detail_notes: Task subsets vary significantly — 14+ different combinations exist across papers. The most common 5-task set (PickCube, StackCube, PickSingleYCB, PickSingleEGAD, PickClutterYCB) covers ~24% of entries. A second common 5-task set (Fill, Hang, PickCube, PickSingleYCB, StackCube) covers ~19%. Scores across different task subsets are not directly comparable. Averaging methods also vary (weighted vs arithmetic). Check notes for which tasks were evaluated. Only entries using the standard 5-task set have a sortable overall score — other task subsets are shown but not ranked.

rlbench.detail_notes: 18-task (PerAct) subset is the dominant standard (~67% of entries). Other common sets: 10-task, 74-task, 249-task. Different task sets are not directly comparable — check notes for task count and variation count. Only 18-task entries have a sortable overall score — other task counts are shown but not ranked.

robocasa.detail_notes: No standardized task subset exists. Papers evaluate on different task subsets and episode counts. No entries currently include task-level breakdown — all report overall scores only. Check notes for what was included.

robotwin_v1.detail_notes: RoboTwin 1.0 (ECCV 2024). Task counts vary by paper (4–17). Scores across different task subsets are not directly comparable.

robotwin_v2.detail_notes: RoboTwin 2.0 (2025). Easy = clean scenes; Hard = 5-axis domain randomization. Two training protocols exist — scores across them are not directly comparable. Protocol A (official, 2506.18088): single-task, 50 clean demos — Hard tests OOD generalization (3–10× gaps). Protocol B (Motus-style, 2512.13030): multi-task, 50 clean + 500 DR demos/task — Hard is in-distribution (near-zero gaps). Check entry notes for protocol.

vlabench.detail_notes: Standard: Track 1-4 Progress Score (PS) avg. Official 6-track system from OpenMOSS/VLABench: (1) in_distribution, (2) cross_category, (3) common_sense, (4) semantic_instruction, (5) cross_task [open], (6) unseen_texture. Metrics: IS (Intention Score) = approached correct object; PS (Progress Score) = task completion. overall_score = Track 1-4 PS avg. Entries from the original VLABench paper (2412.18194) use a pre-track IS-based protocol and have overall_score=null.

mikasa.detail_notes: MIKASA-Robo (2502.10550). Some scores are third-party reproductions — check notes. Ensure overall score reflects the paper's full aggregate, not a selective subset.

kinetix._note: Benchmark protocol (12 dynamic tasks) defined by the RTC paper (2506.07339), using the Kinetix simulator (2410.23208).
kinetix.detail_notes: 12-task eval protocol from the RTC paper (2506.07339). State-based control — no vision or language input. Scores depend on (inference_delay, execution_horizon) settings. Not the Kinetix simulator — it is a specific evaluation protocol.

robocerebra.detail_notes: Includes both end-to-end VLAs and hierarchical systems (VLM planner + controller) — not directly comparable. Typical scores: 5–20%. Small absolute differences may be meaningful.

roboarena._note: Elo-based pairwise arena ranking from distributed real-robot A/B evaluations across 7 institutions using DROID platform
roboarena.detail_notes: RoboArena (2506.18123). Elo-based ranking via pairwise human evaluation of robot manipulation policies.

robochallenge._note: 30 real-robot tasks across 4 platforms (UR5, Franka, ARX5, ALOHA). Progress score (0-100) also reported.
robochallenge.detail_notes: RoboChallenge (2510.17950). Scores synced from official leaderboard API. Manual paper extractions are not accepted.
